_readme: Edit this file to update the portfolio. Push to GitLab, pull on server. No HTML changes needed.

meta.name: Chandrashekhar Bhosale
meta.description: Software Engineer with 8+ years in backend engineering and automation. Creator of DjangoPlay. Pursuing AI Ethics Engineering.

user.summary: With 8+ years of experience for building software across healthcare, education, and automotive domains. Started in automation — learning how systems break and why correctness is non-negotiable. That foundation shapes everything I build now. At Ridecell I chose Python; after that I built DjangoPlay — a production-deployed backend platform with a policy engine, append-only audit log, and fail-closed permissions. The same principles draw me toward AI Ethics Engineering: accountability, traceability, systems that behave predictably under pressure.

pursuing.label: Pursuing
paragraphs: AI systems need the same discipline applied to critical backend infrastructure: auditability so every decision can be explained, fail-closed behaviour so the default is safe, and identity boundaries so no actor exceeds their authority.
My path is to apply this rigour to AI systems — working on infrastructure, tooling, and governance layers that make ML pipelines transparent, accountable, and correctable.

pillars.name: Auditability
pillars.sub: Every decision attributable. Append-only. Built into DjangoPlay from day one.

pillars.name: Fail-Closed Design
pillars.sub: Deny is the default. Missing permission checks are errors, not passes.

pillars.name: Identity Boundaries
pillars.sub: No actor exceeds authority. Centralised policy, never ad-hoc.

pillars.name: Correctness First
pillars.sub: Knowing how systems break shapes how I build systems that don't.

timeline.heading: From understanding how systems break — to building systems that don't.

items.desc: Built a production-deployed Django platform from scratch. 16 domain apps, service layer architecture, centralised policy engine, append-only audit log, JWT + Google SSO, OpenAPI 3, subdomain routing via django-hosts, background task infrastructure with Celery, and 3 PyPI libraries extracted from the work. Deployed on Oracle VPS behind Nginx. GitHub Mirroring via CI/CD pipeline using repository_dispatch events.

The pivot. Chose Python. Backend work on Django microservices for large-scale automotive telematics — integrating third-party hardware providers, shipping REST APIs, debugging Kubernetes workloads on AWS at pod and service level. Deep PostgreSQL-based root-cause analysis on production issues, observability via Grafana and Sentry. Led PoCs, mentored juniors, reviewed backend architecture.

Built CI-integrated automation and performance pipelines for a distributed static code analysis platform used by multiple engineering teams. Worked extensively with Dockerised services and container-based test environments. SQL-driven backend validation with PostgreSQL and SQL Server. Partnered with backend and DevOps teams to improve release safety and deployment confidence.

Validated REST APIs and backend systems supporting large ReactJS applications. MS SQL Server for backend data validation and analysis. Managed CI/CD workflows via Azure DevOps (VSTS). Performance testing with Apache JMeter. First full exposure to the complete software delivery lifecycle.

Started with Java and Selenium at CMD Solutions, automating validation of a real-time public transport system. Moved into data analysis at Jombay: SQL databases, defect tracking, and client feedback loops.

items.org: Applying engineering rigour to AI systems
items.desc: The same principles that make backend systems trustworthy — auditability, fail-closed behaviour, identity boundaries — are precisely what AI systems need. That gap is where I am headed.

paragraphs: A modular backend platform built after leaving full-time employment — a deliberate engineering exercise with no shortcuts. Domain-driven design, service layers, centralised policy engine, append-only audit logging, JWT + Google SSO, subdomain routing, background tasks, and a full OpenAPI 3 schema. Deployed on Oracle VPS behind Nginx.
Three PyPI libraries were extracted from this project: genericissuetracker, djangoplay-cli, and filemerger-cli.

oss_libraries.name: filemerger-cli
oss_libraries.desc: Merges project files into a single text output. Supports human-readable, LLM-optimised, and compact modes. Built for sharing full code context with AI tools.

oss_libraries.name: djangoplay-cli
oss_libraries.desc: Developer CLI for the DjangoPlay platform. Manages local development lifecycle — environment encryption, SSL, Redis, Celery, and server startup in a single command.

oss_libraries.name: genericissuetracker
oss_libraries.desc: Reusable Django issue tracker library. Full CRUD for issues, comments, attachments, labels, and status history. Host-provided identity and permissions — drop-in integration.

repositories.name: djangoplay / djangoplay-web
repositories.desc: Main backend platform — Django, DRF, PostgreSQL, Redis, Celery, Nginx

repositories.name: djangoplay / djangoplay-cli
repositories.desc: Developer CLI — environment management, server startup, Celery orchestration

repositories.name: binaryfleet / issuetracker
repositories.desc: Reusable Django issue tracker library — issues, comments, attachments, labels

repositories.name: binaryfleet / filemerger
repositories.desc: CLI tool for merging project files — human-readable, LLM, and compact modes
